bullets.text: A massive Russian attack on Kyiv has killed at least 27 people, with the latest barrage deploying the largest number of weapons on the capital and hitting locations over a wide area.

Floods in Côte d’Ivoire have killed 59 people since May, with authorities stating that the rainy season is becoming deadlier; Ghana has also reported 13 deaths and floods have impacted Benin, Togo, and Nigeria.

Amnesty International reports that Sudan’s paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) committed crimes against humanity in El Fasher, including ethnic cleansing, as part of a systemic campaign against civilians.

The AFP is investigating allegations of war crimes committed by an Australian citizen serving in the Israel Defense Forces, stemming from claims of deliberate targeting and destruction of residential buildings in Gaza.
A 16-year-old boy has been charged with murder after another boy was found with stab wounds outside a hospital in Craigieburn, Melbourne.
Record rainfall has hit South Australia, with wild winter weather impacting the state and more states expected to be affected.

The US economy saw a slowdown in job growth in June, adding 57,000 jobs, which was below forecasts and followed a three-month period of strong performance. This news has eased concerns about the Federal Reserve needing to raise interest rates soon, leading to gains in most Wall Street stocks.

Inflation, partly attributed to Donald Trump’s tariffs and the Iran war, is expected to make July 4th holiday parties the most expensive ever for Americans, particularly impacting the cost of traditional cookout items.
OpenAI is reportedly in early discussions with the Trump administration to offer a 5% stake in the company, a move that comes amid increasing political pressure on the AI startup.
